sanskrit:
अविभक्तं च भूतेषु विभक्तमिव च स्थितम्।
भूतभर्तृ च तज्ज्ञेयं ग्रसिष्णु प्रभविष्णु च।।
transliteration:
avibhaktaṃ ca bhūteṣu vibhaktam iva ca sthitam |
bhūtabhartṛ ca taj jñeyaṃ grasiṣṇu prabhaviṣṇu ca ||
literal_meaning: Undivided in beings, yet appearing divided; the sustainer, devourer and originator of all is to be known as That.
contextual_meaning: The Self appears fragmented across countless minds, yet remains one seamless awareness. It is the silent witness that maintains, dissolves and re-creates every inner drama without ever being touched by it.
psychological_state: Post-peak-experience integration: the mind has tasted boundless awareness and now struggles to reconcile oneness with ordinary multiplicity.
trigger_scenario: After a deep meditation retreat you open your phone to 73 unread messages; each contact feels like a separate self demanding attention, yet you sense one continuous awareness reading them all.
anchor_text: One silent awareness lives in all, untouched by the play it sustains, dissolves and re-creates.
